rationale: The cited paper is used for a specific functional role, not just background mention. In the theory section and especially Experiment 2, the authors explicitly rely on Novemsky et al. (2007) to justify a font-based preference-fluency manipulation as a way to vary perceived choice difficulty independently of trade-off size. They then use that borrowed manipulation to test their depletion account, concluding that fluency-based difficulty does not produce the same depletion effect as trade-off conflict.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Novemsky et al. (2007)'s preference-fluency manipulation (easy vs difficult-to-read information) to create subjective choice difficulty in a new research context. This lets the authors separate fluency-based difficulty from trade-off conflict when testing depletion in sequential choices.
